# VibeWarden — LLM context index

This file is a machine-readable index of VibeWarden's key concepts, intended
to be included in LLM system prompts or context windows.

---

## What is VibeWarden?

VibeWarden is an open-source security sidecar for vibe-coded apps. It is a
single Go binary (embedding Caddy) that handles TLS, authentication (Ory
Kratos), user management, rate limiting, IP filtering, security headers, egress
proxy, webhook signature verification, and AI-readable structured logs.

The sidecar always runs locally, next to the application, on localhost. It is
never hosted as a service.

Documentation: https://vibewarden.dev/docs

---

## AI-readable log schema

VibeWarden's most original feature is its structured, strongly-typed log schema
designed for consumption by AI agents and LLMs.

Every security-relevant event emitted by the sidecar is a JSON object with:

- `schema_version` — always `"v1"` (stable public API)
- `event_type` — stable dot-separated identifier (e.g. `"auth.success"`)
- `timestamp` — RFC3339 UTC
- `ai_summary` — pre-computed one-sentence description (≤ 200 chars) for LLMs
- `actor` — typed entity that initiated the action (`ip`, `user`, `api_key`, `system`)
- `resource` — what was acted on (`http_endpoint`, `egress_route`, `config`)
- `outcome` — enforcement result (`allowed`, `blocked`, `rate_limited`, `failed`)
- `risk_signals` — machine-detectable threat indicators with normalised scores
- `request_id`, `trace_id`, `triggered_by` — causal chain fields
- `payload` — event-specific structured fields

The schema is published at: https://vibewarden.dev/schema/v1/event.json

Full schema documentation (all event types, field definitions, example events,
and how to adopt the schema in your own app):
https://vibewarden.dev/docs/ai-log-schema/

The MCP tool `vibewarden_schema_describe` provides the same information
programmatically without requiring a live sidecar:

- Called with no arguments: returns a list of all event types with one-line descriptions.
- Called with `event_type=<type>`: returns full field definitions for that event type.

---

## MCP tools

VibeWarden ships a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that AI agents can use
to inspect and manage a local sidecar. Tools:

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `vibewarden_status` | Check whether the sidecar is running |
| `vibewarden_doctor` | Run the full health-check suite |
| `vibewarden_validate` | Validate a vibewarden.yaml configuration file |
| `vibewarden_explain` | Explain a configuration in plain language |
| `vibewarden_prepare_deploy` | Generate a deployment spec for Docker, Railway, or Fly.io |
| `vibewarden_verify_deploy` | Verify a deployed sidecar is reachable and healthy |
| `vibewarden_get_deploy_logs` | Instructions for retrieving sidecar logs |
| `vibewarden_watch_events` | Stream live structured log events from the sidecar |
| `vibewarden_schema_describe` | Describe the AI-readable log schema (pure metadata, no sidecar needed) |
| `vibewarden_propose_action` | Create a configuration-change proposal for human review |
| `vibewarden_list_proposals` | List pending configuration-change proposals |
| `vibewarden_get_proposal` | Get details of a specific proposal |

---

## Configuration

VibeWarden is configured via `vibewarden.yaml`. Key sections:

- `server.port` — port the sidecar listens on (default 8443)
- `upstream.port` — port the upstream application listens on
- `tls.enabled`, `tls.provider` — TLS termination (letsencrypt, self-signed, external)
- `auth.enabled` — Ory Kratos session authentication
- `rate_limit.enabled`, `rate_limit.per_ip` — per-IP rate limiting
- `security_headers.enabled` — HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, etc.
- `egress` — outbound proxy rules with route-level controls
- `plugins.fleet` — opt-in telemetry to the Pro fleet dashboard

---

## Architecture

Hexagonal architecture (ports & adapters) with DDD domain layer.

```
cmd/vibewarden/         main entrypoint
internal/domain/        entities, value objects, domain events (zero external deps)
internal/ports/         interfaces (inbound + outbound)
internal/adapters/      caddy/, kratos/, postgres/, log/, ops/
internal/app/           application services (use cases)
internal/config/        config loading and validation
internal/mcp/           MCP server and tool handlers
internal/plugins/       plugin registry and lifecycle
migrations/             golang-migrate SQL files
docs/                   published documentation (mkdocs site)
```
